dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8532 IH: VA Home Loan Affordability Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Van Orden (for himself, Mr. Bost, Mr. Barrett, Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia, and Mr. Ciscomani) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to align elements of the housing loan program of the Department of Veterans Affairs with requirements of the Federal Housing Administration, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the VA Home Loan Affordability Act.

section.header: Alignment of housing loan program of the Department of Veterans Affairs with requirements of the Federal Housing Administration

subsection.header: Prohibition of requirement of third party verification of lender fees
subsection.text: Section 3703(a)(2)(B) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting in regulations after Secretary may prescribe; and
by adding at the end the following: In prescribing such regulations regarding fees paid by a veteran borrower to lender, the Secretary may not require documentation of such fees by a third party.
Section 3709(a) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (3) as subparagraphs (A) through (C), respectively;
by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: The Secretary may authorize the refinancing of a loan described in paragraph (1) without an appraisal.

paragraph.header: Minimum interest rate for adjustable rate mortgage
paragraph.text: Section 3709(b)(3) of title 38, United States Code, is amended by striking 200 basis points and inserting 75 basis points.

subsection.header: Expansion of guaranteed loans for condominiums
subsection.text: Section 3710(a)(6) of title 38, United States Code, is amended, in subsection (a)(6), by striking , if such development or project is approved by the Secretary under criteria which the Secretary shall prescribe in regulations.

subsection.header: Maximum closing costs and seller fees for guaranteed loans
subsection.text: Section 3710(b) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (7)(C)(ii), by striking ; and and inserting a semicolon;
in paragraph (8), by striking the period at the end and inserting a semicolon; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraphs:
the closing costs actually paid by the veteran do not exceed 1.5 percent of the amount of the loan; and
the seller fees actually paid by the veteran do not exceed six percent of the outstanding balance of the loan.

subsection.text: Section 3710(g)(3)(A), of title 38, United States Code, is amended by inserting , reviewed and prescribed not less than once every two years, after debt-to-income ratios.

subsection.header: Minimum experience of an appraiser required
subsection.text: Section 3731(a)(1) of title 38, United States Code, is amended by striking certification of an appropriate number of years and inserting a certificate or license issued by a State.

subsection.header: Review of appraisal minimum property requirements
subsection.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall—

paragraph.text: review regulations prescribed under section 3710(b)(4) of title 38, United States Code, regarding the suitability of property; and
prescribe new such regulations that the Secretary determines appropriate.

subsection.header: Plan To modernize IT for housing loans
subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall submit to the Committees on Veterans Affairs of the Senate and House of Representatives the plan of the Secretary to modernize the information technology used to administer housing loans under chapter 37 of title 38, United States Code.
